title: crataegus laevigata var. integrifolia
intro: Crataegus laevigata var. integrifolia, also known as the Midland hawthorn, is a variety of hawthorn tree native to Europe, particularly in the UK. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree with medicinal properties, primarily derived from its berries, leaves, and flowers. The medicinal properties of Crataegus laevigata var. integrifolia are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiovascular benefits. The health benefits of this plant include reducing blood pressure, improving heart health, and preventing cardiovascular disease, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and capsules.
